answer: Plaid provides two official Model Context Protocol (MCP) servers [1][2]. The Dashboard MCP server is a remote, production-focused tool for diagnostics and analytics, accessible at `https://api.dashboard.plaid.com/mcp/` using the Streamable HTTP protocol [1][2]. It requires an OAuth token with the `mcp:dashboard` scope [2]. The second is a local Plaid AI coding toolkit for development, which runs as a subprocess to provide mock data, documentation search, and sandbox utilities [1][2]. Numerous independent, third-party MCP servers also exist to bridge Plaid's financial APIs with AI clients like Claude Desktop [3][4].

results.title: The Plaid internal MCP server. Maximizing the leverage of internal AI… | by Plaid Eng | Plaid Engineering
